paragraph: Single-family homes dominate River Vale's market with 30 sales over the last six months at a median of $1,150,000. Sellers are dictating terms here. Two-thirds of homes sold above asking, the sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.03, and the median time on market is 14 days. This is a real seller's market where priced homes move fast and buyers often face multiple offer situations. The range is wide, from $625,000 to $3,400,000, which means neighborhoods and condition matter enormously. Condos and townhouses are present but sparse with 19 sales at a median of $750,000. These move more slowly at 21 days on market, and only 42 percent sold above asking, so buyers have more room to negotiate in this tier. If you're weighing single-family against condo here, the deciding factor isn't market temperature. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family home in what's currently a seller's market, or the lower price point and potentially easier entry terms of a condo where you'll have more negotiating room.
